name: Veles Extended
description: Veles, in Slavic mythology, is a complex and multifaceted deity associated with cattle, wealth, magic, trickery, the underworld, and commerce. He is often portrayed as the antithesis of Perun, the god of thunder and lightning, representing the chthonic and chaotic forces in opposition to Perun's order and celestial power. Veles is seen as a protector of shepherds and livestock, ensuring their fertility and prosperity. He is also believed to control the flow of wealth and abundance, acting as a patron of merchants and traders. His connection to magic and the underworld grants him knowledge of hidden secrets and the ability to influence the unseen realms. Veles is known for his shape-shifting abilities, often appearing as a serpent, dragon, or a bear. While sometimes depicted as a mischievous trickster, he is also revered for his wisdom and connection to the natural world. He represents the untamed wilderness, the cycles of life and death, and the potential for transformation. Veles is associated with music, poetry, and storytelling, inspiring creativity and artistic expression. He is a vital figure in Slavic cosmology, bridging the gap between the earthly and spiritual realms, and embodying the duality of order and chaos that shapes the world. His influence extends to agriculture, divination, and healing, reflecting his deep connection to the land and its resources. Veles guides souls to the afterlife and ensures the continuous cycle of reincarnation.
subtitle: God of Magic, Cattle, and the Underworld

keyMyths.title: Veles and Perun's Conflict
keyMyths.description: Veles steals Perun's cattle and children, leading to a cosmic battle between order and chaos. Perun eventually defeats Veles, restoring balance to the world.

keyMyths.title: Veles as a Shape-Shifter
keyMyths.description: Veles is known for his ability to transform into various animals, often appearing as a serpent, dragon, or bear. This reflects his connection to the wild and untamed aspects of nature.

keyMyths.title: Veles as Guide of Souls
keyMyths.description: Veles guides souls to Nav, the Slavic underworld, ensuring the cycle of death and rebirth continues. He is a psychopomp, leading the deceased through the spiritual realm.

sources.author: Helmold of Bosau
